text: Audit repository metadata and public-facing package manifests
description: Check root and workspace metadata for open source publishing readiness: name, description, license, repository URLs, private flags where intended, and obviously accidental dependency or manifest noise. The repo surface should look intentional before publishing.

reflections.context: Make the repository metadata coherent and safe for a public local-first OSS release.
reflections.outcome: Cleaned the root and workspace manifests, added a top-level ISC LICENSE, marked internal workspaces private, and removed misleading root package runtime dependencies.
reflections.effectiveness: A single audit pass across all manifests was efficient because the problems were repetitive and could be normalized consistently.
reflections.lesson: For repo-first open source projects, private workspace manifests with clear descriptions and repository links are better than leaving packages looking accidentally publishable.
reflections.pattern: This matches earlier release-prep work where repository surface clarity matters more than adding new features.

status.comment: Audited root and workspace manifests for repo publishability. Added intentional descriptions, repository metadata, private flags for internal workspaces, removed accidental root runtime dependencies, and added a top-level ISC LICENSE.
